_comment: Component PURL coordinate -> endoflife.date product, for offline end-of-life (EOL) flagging (enrich-eol.sh). A component whose purl starts with a rule's `purlPrefix` is mapped to that endoflife product; its version's leading segments form the release `cycle` (per the rule's `cycle` granularity), which is looked up in the bundled endoflife dataset (eol-data.json) to decide EOL. This is a CLOSED, curated whitelist on purpose: an unmapped component gets bomlens:eol=unknown, never a guess. Match is by PURL, not display name, because one product arrives under many component names (e.g. spring-boot-starter-web, spring-boot-autoconfigure all map to spring-boot). endoflife.date coverage is runtimes/frameworks/OS, so long-tail libraries are intentionally absent. Keep this map and eol-data.json's product set in sync.
_cycle: cycle granularity: 'major' uses the first numeric version segment (express 4.18.2 -> '4'); 'major.minor' uses the first two joined by '.' (spring-boot 3.2.0 -> '3.2'). Must match how endoflife.date keys that product's cycles.
